content: On November 13, a man came in around 1:30 PM and bought a coffee. He left his phone on the table. I called out, but he didn’t come back. I kept it at the counter. Next day, a woman came asking about it—said it was her brother’s. She gave me her Aadhaar and took it. I didn’t ask questions. I don’t know who he was. On November 15, I saw the same man again—beard, dark coat, no glasses—using a phone at 1:45 AM near the back entrance. He was on the Wi-Fi. I didn’t recognize him. I didn’t think it was important. I didn’t see him again after that.
